visual_description: A close-up shot focuses on two men sitting side-by-side in a dimly lit space, likely the interior of a car at night. The man in the foreground, on the left, is in profile, looking towards the right. He has short dark hair and is wearing a black shirt. The man in the background, on the right, is slightly out of focus. He also has dark hair and is wearing a dark top. The lighting is low, casting soft shadows on their faces. In the top left corner of the screen, the white Chinese characters "风行网" are visible. The camera remains steady, maintaining a tight focus on the man in the foreground. A man speaks in a low, conversational tone, recounting a story with a slightly weary and thoughtful pace. He begins, "He asked me, 'Ah Qiang, how long have you been with me?'" His voice then shifts to a more resigned and slightly exasperated tone as he concludes, "How could I remember?" The background is quiet, with no discernible music or sound effects, placing all the focus on the man's dialogue. The shot then shifts focus to the man on the right, bringing him into clearer view.
